---
title: "transform_geodesic"
generated: true
description: "Point at fraction `t` along the geodesic from identity to `transform`."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.geometry.algebra`](../nitrix-geometry-algebra.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:transform_geodesic -->
```python
transform_geodesic(transform: Float[Array, 'd1 d1'], t: float) -> Float[Array, 'd1 d1']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:transform_geodesic -->
Point at fraction `t` along the geodesic from identity to `transform`.

Evaluates $\exp(t \cdot \log \mathrm{transform})$ in the true matrix
chart: `t = 0` gives the identity, `t = 1` gives `transform`, and the
half-way point squares back to the whole, $T_{1/2} \, T_{1/2} = T$.
Interpolate between two transforms `A` and `B` as
`A @ transform_geodesic(invert(A) @ B, t)`.  This is an offline op (it
uses `matrix_log`); see [`transform_mean`](../nitrix-geometry-algebra/transform_mean.qmd).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:transform_geodesic -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `transform` | `Float` | `d1` × `d1` | — | `(ndim+1, ndim+1)` homogeneous transform whose geodesic from the identity is traced (its linear block needs positive determinant). |
| `t` | `float` | — | — | Scalar fraction along the geodesic; `0` is the identity and `1` is `transform`. Values outside `[0, 1]` extrapolate along the same geodesic. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `d1` × `d1`. The interpolated homogeneous transform, of shape `(ndim+1, ndim+1)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:transform_geodesic -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `d1` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
